reasoning: The FRED statistical analysis estimates a 30% chance of an increase in the short term, due to a visible downward trend over the past few months (from 6.36 in April 2025 down to 6.03 in late August). Additionally, 6.03 is near the upper bound of the very recent trading range (5.95-6.05 in August), suggesting a short-term reversion downwards is more likely than a breakout upwards. Since macroeconomic expectations generally expect easing or moderation, yields are more inclined to trend downward or stay stable barring an unexpected inflation shock. As the forecast horizon extends, uncertainty increases, pulling probabilities steadily closer to 0.5 (0.35 -> 0.38 -> 0.42 -> 0.46).
